entries.title: SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec
entries.summary: SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec is a description of the access request.  Exactly one of resourceAttributes and nonResourceAttributes must be set

entries.title: SelfSubjectRulesReview
entries.summary: SelfSubjectRulesReview enumerates the set of actions the current user can perform within a namespace. The returned list of actions may be incomplete depending on the server's authorization mode, and any errors experienced during the evaluation. SelfSubjectRulesReview should be used by UIs to show/hide actions, or to quickly let an end user reason about their permissions. It should NOT Be used by external systems to drive authorization decisions as this raises confused deputy, cache lifetime/revocation, and correctness concerns. SubjectAccessReview, and LocalAccessReview are the correct way to defer authorization decisions to the API server.

entries.title: SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec
entries.summary: SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec defines the specification for SelfSubjectRulesReview.

entries.title: SubjectAccessReview
entries.summary: SubjectAccessReview checks whether or not a user or group can perform an action.

entries.title: SubjectAccessReviewSpec
entries.summary: SubjectAccessReviewSpec is a description of the access request.  Exactly one of resourceAttributes and nonResourceAttributes must be set

entries.title: SubjectAccessReviewStatus
entries.summary: Represents the result of a SubjectAccessReview request.

entries.title: SubjectRulesReviewStatus
entries.summary: SubjectRulesReviewStatus contains the result of a rules check. This check can be incomplete depending on the set of authorizers the server is configured with and any errors experienced during evaluation. Because authorization rules are additive, if a rule appears in a list it's safe to assume the subject has that permission, even if that list is incomplete.

entries.title: CrossVersionObjectReference
entries.summary: CrossVersionObjectReference contains enough information to let you identify the referred resource.

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
entries.summary: configuration of a horizontal pod autoscaler.

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscalerList
entries.summary: list of horizontal pod autoscaler objects.

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec
entries.summary: specification of a horizontal pod autoscaler.

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscalerStatus
entries.summary: current status of a horizontal pod autoscaler

entries.title: Scale
entries.summary: Scale represents a scaling request for a resource.

entries.title: ScaleSpec
entries.summary: ScaleSpec describes the attributes of a scale subresource.

entries.title: ScaleStatus
entries.summary: ScaleStatus represents the current status of a scale subresource.

entries.title: ContainerResourceMetricSource
entries.summary: ContainerResourceMetricSource indicates how to scale on a resource metric known to Kubernetes, as specified in requests and limits, describing each pod in the current scale target (e.g. CPU or memory).  The values will be averaged together before being compared to the target.  Such metrics are built in to Kubernetes, and have special scaling options on top of those available to normal per-pod metrics using the "pods" source.  Only one "target" type should be set.

entries.title: ContainerResourceMetricStatus
entries.summary: ContainerResourceMetricStatus indicates the current value of a resource metric known to Kubernetes, as specified in requests and limits, describing a single container in each pod in the current scale target (e.g. CPU or memory).  Such metrics are built in to Kubernetes, and have special scaling options on top of those available to normal per-pod metrics using the "pods" source.

entries.title: ExternalMetricSource
entries.summary: ExternalMetricSource indicates how to scale on a metric not associated with any Kubernetes object (for example length of queue in cloud messaging service, or QPS from loadbalancer running outside of cluster).

entries.title: ExternalMetricStatus
entries.summary: ExternalMetricStatus indicates the current value of a global metric not associated with any Kubernetes object.

entries.title: HPAScalingPolicy
entries.summary: HPAScalingPolicy is a single policy which must hold true for a specified past interval.

entries.title: HPAScalingRules
entries.summary:
HPAScalingRules configures the scaling behavior for one direction via scaling Policy Rules and a configurable metric tolerance.

Scaling Policy Rules are applied after calculating DesiredReplicas from metrics for the HPA. They can limit the scaling velocity by specifying scaling policies. They can prevent flapping by specifying the stabilization window, so that the number of replicas is not set instantly, instead, the safest value from the stabilization window is chosen.

The tolerance is applied to the metric values and prevents scaling too eagerly for small metric variations.

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscalerBehavior
entries.summary: HorizontalPodAutoscalerBehavior configures the scaling behavior of the target in both Up and Down directions (scaleUp and scaleDown fields respectively).

entries.title: HorizontalPodAutoscalerCondition
entries.summary: HorizontalPodAutoscalerCondition describes the state of a HorizontalPodAutoscaler at a certain point.

entries.title: MetricIdentifier
entries.summary: MetricIdentifier defines the name and optionally selector for a metric

entries.title: MetricSpec
entries.summary: MetricSpec specifies how to scale based on a single metric (only `type` and one other matching field should be set at once).

entries.title: MetricStatus
entries.summary: MetricStatus describes the last-read state of a single metric.

entries.title: MetricTarget
entries.summary: MetricTarget defines the target value, average value, or average utilization of a specific metric

entries.title: MetricValueStatus
entries.summary: MetricValueStatus holds the current value for a metric

entries.title: ObjectMetricSource
entries.summary: ObjectMetricSource indicates how to scale on a metric describing a kubernetes object (for example, hits-per-second on an Ingress object).

entries.title: ObjectMetricStatus
entries.summary: ObjectMetricStatus indicates the current value of a metric describing a kubernetes object (for example, hits-per-second on an Ingress object).

entries.title: PodsMetricSource
entries.summary: PodsMetricSource indicates how to scale on a metric describing each pod in the current scale target (for example, transactions-processed-per-second). The values will be averaged together before being compared to the target value.

entries.title: PodsMetricStatus
entries.summary: PodsMetricStatus indicates the current value of a metric describing each pod in the current scale target (for example, transactions-processed-per-second).

entries.title: ResourceMetricSource
entries.summary: ResourceMetricSource indicates how to scale on a resource metric known to Kubernetes, as specified in requests and limits, describing each pod in the current scale target (e.g. CPU or memory).  The values will be averaged together before being compared to the target.  Such metrics are built in to Kubernetes, and have special scaling options on top of those available to normal per-pod metrics using the "pods" source.  Only one "target" type should be set.

entries.title: ResourceMetricStatus
entries.summary: ResourceMetricStatus indicates the current value of a resource metric known to Kubernetes, as specified in requests and limits, describing each pod in the current scale target (e.g. CPU or memory).  Such metrics are built in to Kubernetes, and have special scaling options on top of those available to normal per-pod metrics using the "pods" source.

entries.title: CronJob
entries.summary: CronJob represents the configuration of a single cron job.

entries.title: CronJobList
entries.summary: CronJobList is a collection of cron jobs.

entries.title: CronJobSpec
entries.summary: CronJobSpec describes how the job execution will look like and when it will actually run.

entries.title: CronJobStatus
entries.summary: CronJobStatus represents the current state of a cron job.

entries.title: Job
entries.summary: Job represents the configuration of a single job.

entries.title: JobCondition
entries.summary: JobCondition describes current state of a job.

entries.title: JobList
entries.summary: A list of Jobs.

entries.title: JobSpec
entries.summary: JobSpec describes how the job execution will look like.

entries.title: JobStatus
entries.summary: JobStatus represents the current state of a Job.

entries.title: JobTemplateSpec
entries.summary: JobTemplateSpec describes the data a Job should have when created from a template

entries.title: PodFailurePolicy
entries.summary: PodFailurePolicy describes how failed pods influence the backoffLimit.

entries.title: PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesRequirement
entries.summary: PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesRequirement describes the requirement for handling a failed pod based on its container exit codes. In particular, it lookups the .state.terminated.exitCode for each app container and init container status, represented by the .status.containerStatuses and .status.initContainerStatuses fields in the Pod status, respectively. Containers completed with success (exit code 0) are excluded from the requirement check.

entries.title: PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern
entries.summary: PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern describes a pattern for matching an actual pod condition type.

entries.title: PodFailurePolicyRule
entries.summary: PodFailurePolicyRule describes how a pod failure is handled when the requirements are met. One of onExitCodes and onPodConditions, but not both, can be used in each rule.

entries.title: SuccessPolicy
entries.summary: SuccessPolicy describes when a Job can be declared as succeeded based on the success of some indexes.

entries.title: SuccessPolicyRule
entries.summary: SuccessPolicyRule describes rule for declaring a Job as succeeded. Each rule must have at least one of the "succeededIndexes" or "succeededCount" specified.

entries.title: UncountedTerminatedPods
entries.summary: UncountedTerminatedPods holds UIDs of Pods that have terminated but haven't been accounted in Job status counters.

entries.title: CertificateSigningRequest
entries.summary:
CertificateSigningRequest objects provide a mechanism to obtain x509 certificates by submitting a certificate signing request, and having it asynchronously approved and issued.

Kubelets use this API to obtain:
 1. client certificates to authenticate to kube-apiserver (with the "kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client-kubelet" signerName).
 2. serving certificates for TLS endpoints kube-apiserver can connect to securely (with the "kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving" signerName).

This API can be used to request client certificates to authenticate to kube-apiserver (with the "kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client" signerName), or to obtain certificates from custom non-Kubernetes signers.

entries.title: CertificateSigningRequestCondition
entries.summary: CertificateSigningRequestCondition describes a condition of a CertificateSigningRequest object

entries.title: CertificateSigningRequestList
entries.summary: CertificateSigningRequestList is a collection of CertificateSigningRequest objects

entries.title: CertificateSigningRequestSpec
entries.summary: CertificateSigningRequestSpec contains the certificate request.

entries.title: CertificateSigningRequestStatus
entries.summary: CertificateSigningRequestStatus contains conditions used to indicate approved/denied/failed status of the request, and the issued certificate.

entries.title: ClusterTrustBundle
entries.summary:
ClusterTrustBundle is a cluster-scoped container for X.509 trust anchors (root certificates).

ClusterTrustBundle objects are considered to be readable by any authenticated user in the cluster, because they can be mounted by pods using the `clusterTrustBundle` projection.  All service accounts have read access to ClusterTrustBundles by default.  Users who only have namespace-level access to a cluster can read ClusterTrustBundles by impersonating a serviceaccount that they have access to.

It can be optionally associated with a particular signer, in which case it contains one valid set of trust anchors for that signer. Signers may have multiple associated ClusterTrustBundles; each is an independent set of trust anchors for that signer. Admission control is used to enforce that only users with permissions on the signer can create or modify the corresponding bundle.

entries.title: ClusterTrustBundleList
entries.summary: ClusterTrustBundleList is a collection of ClusterTrustBundle objects

entries.title: ClusterTrustBundleSpec
entries.summary: ClusterTrustBundleSpec contains the signer and trust anchors.

entries.title: PodCertificateRequest
entries.summary:
PodCertificateRequest encodes a pod requesting a certificate from a given signer.

Kubelets use this API to implement podCertificate projected volumes

entries.title: PodCertificateRequestList
entries.summary: PodCertificateRequestList is a collection of PodCertificateRequest objects

entries.title: PodCertificateRequestSpec
entries.summary: PodCertificateRequestSpec describes the certificate request.  All fields are immutable after creation.

entries.title: PodCertificateRequestStatus
entries.summary: PodCertificateRequestStatus describes the status of the request, and holds the certificate data if the request is issued.

entries.title: Lease
entries.summary: A Lease object represents a lock on a key in etcd.

entries.title: LeaseList
entries.summary: A LeaseList is a list of Lease objects in Kubernetes.

entries.title: LeaseSpec
entries.summary: LeaseSpec is a specification of a Lease.

entries.title: LeaseCandidate
entries.summary: LeaseCandidate defines a candidate for a Lease object. Candidates are created such that coordinated leader election will pick the best leader from the list of candidates.

entries.title: LeaseCandidateList
entries.summary: LeaseCandidateList is a list of Lease objects.

entries.title: LeaseCandidateSpec
entries.summary: LeaseCandidateSpec is a specification of a Lease.

entries.title: AWSElasticBlockStoreVolumeSource
entries.summary:
Represents a Persistent Disk resource in AWS.

An AWS EBS disk must exist before mounting to a container. The disk must also be in the same AWS zone as the kubelet. An AWS EBS disk can only be mounted as read/write once. AWS EBS volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: Affinity
entries.summary: Affinity is a group of affinity scheduling rules.

entries.title: AppArmorProfile
entries.summary: AppArmorProfile defines a pod or container's AppArmor settings.

entries.title: AttachedVolume
entries.summary: AttachedVolume describes a volume attached to a node

entries.title: AzureDiskVolumeSource
entries.summary: AzureDisk represents an Azure Data Disk mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.

entries.title: AzureFilePersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: AzureFile represents an Azure File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.

entries.title: AzureFileVolumeSource
entries.summary: AzureFile represents an Azure File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.

entries.title: Binding
entries.summary: Binding ties one object to another; for example, a pod is bound to a node by a scheduler.

entries.title: CSIPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents storage that is managed by an external CSI volume driver

entries.title: CSIVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a source location of a volume to mount, managed by an external CSI driver

entries.title: Capabilities
entries.summary: Adds and removes POSIX capabilities from running containers.

entries.title: CephFSPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Ceph Filesystem mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod Cephfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: CephFSVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Ceph Filesystem mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod Cephfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: CinderPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a cinder volume resource in Openstack. A Cinder volume must exist before mounting to a container. The volume must also be in the same region as the kubelet. Cinder volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: CinderVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a cinder volume resource in Openstack. A Cinder volume must exist before mounting to a container. The volume must also be in the same region as the kubelet. Cinder volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: ClientIPConfig
entries.summary: ClientIPConfig represents the configurations of Client IP based session affinity.

entries.title: ClusterTrustBundleProjection
entries.summary: ClusterTrustBundleProjection describes how to select a set of ClusterTrustBundle objects and project their contents into the pod filesystem.

entries.title: ComponentCondition
entries.summary: Information about the condition of a component.

entries.title: ComponentStatus
entries.summary: ComponentStatus (and ComponentStatusList) holds the cluster validation info. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.19+

entries.title: ComponentStatusList
entries.summary: Status of all the conditions for the component as a list of ComponentStatus objects. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.19+

entries.title: ConfigMap
entries.summary: ConfigMap holds configuration data for pods to consume.

entries.title: ConfigMapEnvSource
entries.summary:
ConfigMapEnvSource selects a ConfigMap to populate the environment variables with.

The contents of the target ConfigMap's Data field will represent the key-value pairs as environment variables. Keys in the BinaryData field are not currently propagated to container env vars.

entries.title: ConfigMapKeySelector
entries.summary: A ConfigMapKeySelector is a selector that identifies a key in a ConfigMap.

entries.title: ConfigMapList
entries.summary: ConfigMapList is a resource containing a list of ConfigMap objects.

entries.title: ConfigMapNodeConfigSource
entries.summary: ConfigMapNodeConfigSource contains the information to reference a ConfigMap as a config source for the Node. This API is deprecated since 1.22: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/281-dynamic-kubelet-configuration

entries.title: ConfigMapProjection
entries.summary:
Adapts a ConfigMap into a projected volume.

The contents of the target ConfigMap's Data field will be presented in a projected volume as files using the keys in the Data field as the file names, unless the items element is populated with specific mappings of keys to paths. Note that this is identical to a configmap volume source without the default mode.

entries.title: ConfigMapVolumeSource
entries.summary:
Adapts a ConfigMap into a volume.

The contents of the target ConfigMap's Data field will be presented in a volume as files using the keys in the Data field as the file names, unless the items element is populated with specific mappings of keys to paths. ConfigMap volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: Container
entries.summary: A single application container that you want to run within a pod.

entries.title: ContainerExtendedResourceRequest
entries.summary: ContainerExtendedResourceRequest has the mapping of container name, extended resource name to the device request name.

entries.title: ContainerImage
entries.summary: Represents the image used by a container.

entries.title: ContainerPort
entries.summary: ContainerPort represents a network port in a single container.

entries.title: ContainerResizePolicy
entries.summary: ContainerResizePolicy represents resource resize policy for the container.

entries.title: ContainerRestartRule
entries.summary: ContainerRestartRule describes how a container exit is handled.

entries.title: ContainerRestartRuleOnExitCodes
entries.summary: ContainerRestartRuleOnExitCodes describes the condition for handling an exited container based on its exit codes.

entries.title: ContainerState
entries.summary: ContainerState holds a possible state of container. Only one of its members may be specified. If none of them is specified, the default one is ContainerStateWaiting.

entries.title: ContainerStateRunning
entries.summary: ContainerStateRunning is a running state of a container.

entries.title: ContainerStateTerminated
entries.summary: ContainerStateTerminated is a terminated state of a container.

entries.title: ContainerStateWaiting
entries.summary: ContainerStateWaiting is a waiting state of a container.

entries.title: ContainerStatus
entries.summary: ContainerStatus contains details for the current status of this container.

entries.title: ContainerUser
entries.summary: ContainerUser represents user identity information

entries.title: DaemonEndpoint
entries.summary: DaemonEndpoint contains information about a single Daemon endpoint.

entries.title: DownwardAPIProjection
entries.summary: Represents downward API info for projecting into a projected volume. Note that this is identical to a downwardAPI volume source without the default mode.

entries.title: DownwardAPIVolumeFile
entries.summary: DownwardAPIVolumeFile represents information to create the file containing the pod field

entries.title: DownwardAPIVolumeSource
entries.summary: DownwardAPIVolumeSource represents a volume containing downward API info. Downward API volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: EmptyDirVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents an empty directory for a pod. Empty directory volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: EndpointAddress
entries.summary: EndpointAddress is a tuple that describes single IP address. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.33+.

entries.title: EndpointPort
entries.summary: EndpointPort is a tuple that describes a single port. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.33+.

entries.title: EndpointSubset
entries.summary:
EndpointSubset is a group of addresses with a common set of ports. The expanded set of endpoints is the Cartesian product of Addresses x Ports. For example, given:

	{
	  Addresses: [{"ip": "10.10.1.1"}, {"ip": "10.10.2.2"}],
	  Ports:     [{"name": "a", "port": 8675}, {"name": "b", "port": 309}]
	}

The resulting set of endpoints can be viewed as:

	a: [ 10.10.1.1:8675, 10.10.2.2:8675 ],
	b: [ 10.10.1.1:309, 10.10.2.2:309 ]

Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.33+.

entries.title: Endpoints
EndpointsList
entries.summary: EndpointsList is a list of endpoints. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.33+.

entries.title: EnvFromSource
entries.summary: EnvFromSource represents the source of a set of ConfigMaps or Secrets

entries.title: EnvVar
entries.summary: EnvVar represents an environment variable present in a Container.

entries.title: EnvVarSource
entries.summary: EnvVarSource represents a source for the value of an EnvVar.

entries.title: EphemeralContainer
entries.summary:
An EphemeralContainer is a temporary container that you may add to an existing Pod for user-initiated activities such as debugging. Ephemeral containers have no resource or scheduling guarantees, and they will not be restarted when they exit or when a Pod is removed or restarted. The kubelet may evict a Pod if an ephemeral container causes the Pod to exceed its resource allocation.

To add an ephemeral container, use the ephemeralcontainers subresource of an existing Pod. Ephemeral containers may not be removed or restarted.

entries.title: EphemeralVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents an ephemeral volume that is handled by a normal storage driver.

entries.title: Event
entries.summary: Event is a report of an event somewhere in the cluster.  Events have a limited retention time and triggers and messages may evolve with time.  Event consumers should not rely on the timing of an event with a given Reason reflecting a consistent underlying trigger, or the continued existence of events with that Reason.  Events should be treated as informative, best-effort, supplemental data.

entries.title: EventList
entries.summary: EventList is a list of events.

entries.title: EventSeries
entries.summary: EventSeries contain information on series of events, i.e. thing that was/is happening continuously for some time.

entries.title: EventSource
entries.summary: EventSource contains information for an event.

entries.title: ExecAction
entries.summary: ExecAction describes a "run in container" action.

entries.title: FCVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Fibre Channel volume. Fibre Channel volumes can only be mounted as read/write once. Fibre Channel volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: FileKeySelector
entries.summary: FileKeySelector selects a key of the env file.

entries.title: FlexPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: FlexPersistentVolumeSource represents a generic persistent volume resource that is provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.

entries.title: FlexVolumeSource
entries.summary: FlexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.

entries.title: FlockerVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Flocker volume mounted by the Flocker agent. One and only one of datasetName and datasetUUID should be set. Flocker volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: GCEPersistentDiskVolumeSource
entries.summary:
Represents a Persistent Disk resource in Google Compute Engine.

A GCE PD must exist before mounting to a container. The disk must also be in the same GCE project and zone as the kubelet. A GCE PD can only be mounted as read/write once or read-only many times. GCE PDs support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: GRPCAction
entries.summary: GRPCAction specifies an action involving a GRPC service.

entries.title: GitRepoVolumeSource
entries.summary:
Represents a volume that is populated with the contents of a git repository. Git repo volumes do not support ownership management. Git repo volumes support SELinux relabeling.

DEPRECATED: GitRepo is deprecated. To provision a container with a git repo, mount an EmptyDir into an InitContainer that clones the repo using git, then mount the EmptyDir into the Pod's container.

entries.title: GlusterfsPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: GlusterfsVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: HTTPGetAction
entries.summary: HTTPGetAction describes an action based on HTTP Get requests.

entries.title: HTTPHeader
entries.summary: HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes

entries.title: HostAlias
entries.summary: HostAlias holds the mapping between IP and hostnames that will be injected as an entry in the pod's hosts file.

entries.title: HostIP
entries.summary: HostIP represents a single IP address allocated to the host.

entries.title: HostPathVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a host path mapped into a pod. Host path volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: ISCSIPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: ISCSIPersistentVolumeSource represents an ISCSI disk. ISCSI volumes can only be mounted as read/write once. ISCSI volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: ISCSIVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents an ISCSI disk. ISCSI volumes can only be mounted as read/write once. ISCSI volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: ImageVolumeSource
entries.summary: ImageVolumeSource represents a image volume resource.

entries.title: ImageVolumeStatus
entries.summary: ImageVolumeStatus represents the image-based volume status.

entries.title: KeyToPath
entries.summary: Maps a string key to a path within a volume.

entries.title: Lifecycle
entries.summary: Lifecycle describes actions that the management system should take in response to container lifecycle events. For the PostStart and PreStop lifecycle handlers, management of the container blocks until the action is complete, unless the container process fails, in which case the handler is aborted.

entries.title: LifecycleHandler
entries.summary: LifecycleHandler defines a specific action that should be taken in a lifecycle hook. One and only one of the fields, except TCPSocket must be specified.

entries.title: LimitRange
entries.summary: LimitRange sets resource usage limits for each kind of resource in a Namespace.

entries.title: LimitRangeItem
entries.summary: LimitRangeItem defines a min/max usage limit for any resource that matches on kind.

entries.title: LimitRangeList
entries.summary: LimitRangeList is a list of LimitRange items.

entries.title: LimitRangeSpec
entries.summary: LimitRangeSpec defines a min/max usage limit for resources that match on kind.

entries.title: LinuxContainerUser
entries.summary: LinuxContainerUser represents user identity information in Linux containers

entries.title: LoadBalancerIngress
entries.summary: LoadBalancerIngress represents the status of a load-balancer ingress point: traffic intended for the service should be sent to an ingress point.

entries.title: LoadBalancerStatus
entries.summary: LoadBalancerStatus represents the status of a load-balancer.

entries.title: LocalObjectReference
entries.summary: LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the referenced object inside the same namespace.

entries.title: LocalVolumeSource
entries.summary: Local represents directly-attached storage with node affinity

entries.title: ModifyVolumeStatus
entries.summary: ModifyVolumeStatus represents the status object of ControllerModifyVolume operation

entries.title: NFSVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents an NFS mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. NFS volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: Namespace
entries.summary: Namespace provides a scope for Names. Use of multiple namespaces is optional.

entries.title: NamespaceCondition
entries.summary: NamespaceCondition contains details about state of namespace.

entries.title: NamespaceList
entries.summary: NamespaceList is a Kubernetes API resource that represents a list of namespaces.

entries.title: NamespaceSpec
entries.summary: NamespaceSpec describes the attributes on a Namespace.

entries.title: NamespaceStatus
entries.summary: NamespaceStatus is information about the current status of a Namespace.

entries.title: Node
entries.summary: Node is a worker node in Kubernetes. Each node will have a unique identifier in the cache (i.e. in etcd).

entries.title: NodeAddress
entries.summary: NodeAddress contains information for the node's address.

entries.title: NodeAffinity
entries.summary: Node affinity is a group of node affinity scheduling rules.

entries.title: NodeAllocatableResourceClaimStatus
entries.summary: NodeAllocatableResourceClaimStatus describes the status of node allocatable resources allocated via DRA.

entries.title: NodeCondition
entries.summary: NodeCondition contains condition information for a node.

entries.title: NodeConfigSource
entries.summary: NodeConfigSource specifies a source of node configuration. Exactly one subfield (excluding metadata) must be non-nil. This API is deprecated since 1.22

entries.title: NodeConfigStatus
entries.summary: NodeConfigStatus describes the status of the config assigned by Node.Spec.ConfigSource.

entries.title: NodeDaemonEndpoints
entries.summary: NodeDaemonEndpoints lists ports opened by daemons running on the Node.

entries.title: NodeFeatures
entries.summary: NodeFeatures describes the set of features implemented by the CRI implementation. The features contained in the NodeFeatures should depend only on the cri implementation independent of runtime handlers.

entries.title: NodeList
entries.summary: NodeList is the whole list of all Nodes which have been registered with master.

entries.title: NodeRuntimeHandler
entries.summary: NodeRuntimeHandler is a set of runtime handler information.

entries.title: NodeRuntimeHandlerFeatures
entries.summary: NodeRuntimeHandlerFeatures is a set of features implemented by the runtime handler.

entries.title: NodeSelector
entries.summary: A node selector represents the union of the results of one or more label queries over a set of nodes; that is, it represents the OR of the selectors represented by the node selector terms.

entries.title: NodeSelectorRequirement
entries.summary: A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

entries.title: NodeSelectorTerm
entries.summary: A null or empty node selector term matches no objects. The requirements of them are ANDed. The TopologySelectorTerm type implements a subset of the NodeSelectorTerm.

entries.title: NodeSpec
entries.summary: NodeSpec describes the attributes that a node is created with.

entries.title: NodeStatus
entries.summary: NodeStatus is information about the current status of a node.

entries.title: NodeSwapStatus
entries.summary: NodeSwapStatus represents swap memory information.

entries.title: NodeSystemInfo
entries.summary: NodeSystemInfo is a set of ids/uuids to uniquely identify the node.

entries.title: ObjectFieldSelector
entries.summary: ObjectFieldSelector selects an APIVersioned field of an object.

entries.title: ObjectReference
entries.summary: ObjectReference contains enough information to let you inspect or modify the referred object.

entries.title: PersistentVolume
entries.summary: PersistentVolume (PV) is a storage resource provisioned by an administrator. It is analogous to a node. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaim
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaim is a user's request for and claim to a persistent volume

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimCondition
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimCondition contains details about state of pvc

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimList
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimList is a list of PersistentVolumeClaim items.

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimSpec
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimSpec describes the common attributes of storage devices and allows a Source for provider-specific attributes

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimStatus
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimStatus is the current status of a persistent volume claim.

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimTemplate
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimTemplate is used to produce PersistentVolumeClaim objects as part of an EphemeralVolumeSource.

entries.title: PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource references the user's PVC in the same namespace. This volume finds the bound PV and mounts that volume for the pod. A PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource is, essentially, a wrapper around another type of volume that is owned by someone else (the system).

entries.title: PersistentVolumeList
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeList is a list of PersistentVolume items.

entries.title: PersistentVolumeSpec
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeSpec is the specification of a persistent volume.

entries.title: PersistentVolumeStatus
entries.summary: PersistentVolumeStatus is the current status of a persistent volume.

entries.title: PhotonPersistentDiskVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Photon Controller persistent disk resource.

entries.title: Pod
entries.summary: Pod is a collection of containers that can run on a host. This resource is created by clients and scheduled onto hosts.

entries.title: PodAffinity
entries.summary: Pod affinity is a group of inter pod affinity scheduling rules.

entries.title: PodAffinityTerm
entries.summary: Defines a set of pods (namely those matching the labelSelector relative to the given namespace(s)) that this pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key <topologyKey> matches that of any node on which a pod of the set of pods is running

entries.title: PodAntiAffinity
entries.summary: Pod anti affinity is a group of inter pod anti affinity scheduling rules.

entries.title: PodCertificateProjection
entries.summary: PodCertificateProjection provides a private key and X.509 certificate in the pod filesystem.

entries.title: PodCondition
entries.summary: PodCondition contains details for the current condition of this pod.

entries.title: PodDNSConfig
entries.summary: PodDNSConfig defines the DNS parameters of a pod in addition to those generated from DNSPolicy.

entries.title: PodDNSConfigOption
entries.summary: PodDNSConfigOption defines DNS resolver options of a pod.

entries.title: PodExtendedResourceClaimStatus
entries.summary: PodExtendedResourceClaimStatus is stored in the PodStatus for the extended resource requests backed by DRA. It stores the generated name for the corresponding special ResourceClaim created by the scheduler.

entries.title: PodIP
entries.summary: PodIP represents a single IP address allocated to the pod.

entries.title: PodList
entries.summary: A list of Pods within a Kubernetes cluster.

entries.title: PodOS
entries.summary: PodOS defines the OS parameters of a pod.

entries.title: PodReadinessGate
entries.summary: PodReadinessGate contains the reference to a pod condition

entries.title: PodResourceClaim
entries.summary:
PodResourceClaim references exactly one ResourceClaim, either directly or by naming a ResourceClaimTemplate which is then turned into a ResourceClaim for the pod.

It adds a name to it that uniquely identifies the ResourceClaim inside the Pod. Containers that need access to the ResourceClaim reference it with this name.

When the DRAWorkloadResourceClaims feature gate is enabled and this Pod belongs to a PodGroup, a PodResourceClaim is matched to a PodGroupResourceClaim if all of their fields are equal (Name, ResourceClaimName, and ResourceClaimTemplateName). A matched claim references a single ResourceClaim shared across all Pods in the PodGroup, reserved for the PodGroup in ResourceClaimStatus.ReservedFor rather than for individual Pods.

entries.title: PodResourceClaimStatus
entries.summary: PodResourceClaimStatus is stored in the PodStatus for each PodResourceClaim which references a ResourceClaimTemplate. It stores the generated name for the corresponding ResourceClaim.

entries.title: PodSchedulingGate
entries.summary: PodSchedulingGate is associated to a Pod to guard its scheduling.

entries.title: PodSchedulingGroup
entries.summary: PodSchedulingGroup identifies the runtime scheduling group instance that a Pod belongs to. The scheduler uses this information to apply workload-aware scheduling semantics. Exactly one field must be specified.

entries.title: PodSecurityContext
entries.summary: PodSecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. Some fields are also present in container.securityContext.  Field values of container.securityContext take precedence over field values of PodSecurityContext.

entries.title: PodSpec
entries.summary: A PodSpec is used to define the desired state of a pod.

entries.title: PodStatus
entries.summary: PodStatus represents information about the status of a pod. Status may trail the actual state of a system, especially if the node that hosts the pod cannot contact the control plane.

entries.title: PodTemplate
entries.summary: PodTemplate describes a template for creating copies of a predefined pod.

entries.title: PodTemplateList
entries.summary: PodTemplateList is a list of PodTemplates.

entries.title: PodTemplateSpec
entries.summary: PodTemplateSpec describes the data a pod should have when created from a template

entries.title: PortStatus
entries.summary: PortStatus represents the error condition of a service port

entries.title: PortworxVolumeSource
entries.summary: PortworxVolumeSource represents a Portworx volume resource.

entries.title: PreferredSchedulingTerm
entries.summary: An empty preferred scheduling term matches all objects with implicit weight 0 (i.e. it's a no-op). A null preferred scheduling term matches no objects (i.e. is also a no-op).

entries.title: Probe
entries.summary: Probe describes a health check to be performed against a container to determine whether it is alive or ready to receive traffic.

entries.title: ProjectedVolumeSource
entries.summary: A ProjectedVolumeSource is a volume source that projects a volume from one namespace to another.

entries.title: QuobyteVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Quobyte mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. Quobyte volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: RBDPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Rados Block Device mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. RBD volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: RBDVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a Rados Block Device mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod. RBD volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: ReplicationController
entries.summary: ReplicationController represents the configuration of a replication controller.

entries.title: ReplicationControllerCondition
entries.summary: ReplicationControllerCondition describes the state of a replication controller at a certain point.

entries.title: ReplicationControllerList
entries.summary: ReplicationControllerList is a collection of replication controllers.

entries.title: ReplicationControllerSpec
entries.summary: ReplicationControllerSpec is the specification of a replication controller.

entries.title: ReplicationControllerStatus
entries.summary: ReplicationControllerStatus represents the current status of a replication controller.

entries.title: ResourceClaim
entries.summary: ResourceClaim references one entry in PodSpec.ResourceClaims.

entries.title: ResourceFieldSelector
entries.summary: ResourceFieldSelector represents container resources (cpu, memory) and their output format

entries.title: ResourceHealth
entries.summary: ResourceHealth represents the health of a resource. It has the latest device health information. This is a part of KEP https://kep.k8s.io/4680.

entries.title: ResourceQuota
entries.summary: ResourceQuota sets aggregate quota restrictions enforced per namespace

entries.title: ResourceQuotaList
entries.summary: ResourceQuotaList is a list of ResourceQuota items.

entries.title: ResourceQuotaSpec
entries.summary: ResourceQuotaSpec defines the desired hard limits to enforce for Quota.

entries.title: ResourceQuotaStatus
entries.summary: ResourceQuotaStatus defines the enforced hard limits and observed use.

entries.title: ResourceRequirements
entries.summary: ResourceRequirements describes the compute resource requirements.

entries.title: ResourceStatus
entries.summary: ResourceStatus represents the status of a single resource allocated to a Pod.

entries.title: SELinuxOptions
entries.summary: SELinuxOptions are the labels to be applied to the container

entries.title: ScaleIOPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: ScaleIOPersistentVolumeSource represents a persistent ScaleIO volume

entries.title: ScaleIOVolumeSource
entries.summary: ScaleIOVolumeSource represents a persistent ScaleIO volume

entries.title: ScopeSelector
entries.summary: A scope selector represents the AND of the selectors represented by the scoped-resource selector requirements.

entries.title: ScopedResourceSelectorRequirement
entries.summary: A scoped-resource selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a scope name, and an operator that relates the scope name and values.

entries.title: SeccompProfile
entries.summary: SeccompProfile defines a pod/container's seccomp profile settings. Only one profile source may be set.

entries.title: Secret
entries.summary: Secret holds secret data of a certain type. The total bytes of the values in the Data field must be less than MaxSecretSize bytes.

entries.title: SecretEnvSource
entries.summary:
SecretEnvSource selects a Secret to populate the environment variables with.

The contents of the target Secret's Data field will represent the key-value pairs as environment variables.

entries.title: SecretKeySelector
entries.summary: SecretKeySelector selects a key of a Secret.

entries.title: SecretList
entries.summary: A list of secrets.

entries.title: SecretProjection
entries.summary:
Adapts a secret into a projected volume.

The contents of the target Secret's Data field will be presented in a projected volume as files using the keys in the Data field as the file names. Note that this is identical to a secret volume source without the default mode.

entries.title: SecretReference
entries.summary: SecretReference represents a Secret Reference. It has enough information to retrieve secret in any namespace

entries.title: SecretVolumeSource
entries.summary:
Adapts a Secret into a volume.

The contents of the target Secret's Data field will be presented in a volume as files using the keys in the Data field as the file names. Secret volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.

entries.title: SecurityContext
entries.summary: SecurityContext holds security configuration that will be applied to a container. Some fields are present in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext.  When both are set, the values in SecurityContext take precedence.

entries.title: Service
entries.summary: Service is a named abstraction of software service (for example, mysql) consisting of local port (for example 3306) that the proxy listens on, and the selector that determines which pods will answer requests sent through the proxy.

entries.title: ServiceAccount
entries.summary: ServiceAccount binds together: * a name, understood by users, and perhaps by peripheral systems, for an identity * a principal that can be authenticated and authorized * a set of secrets

entries.title: ServiceAccountList
entries.summary: ServiceAccountList is a list of ServiceAccount objects

entries.title: ServiceAccountTokenProjection
entries.summary: ServiceAccountTokenProjection represents a projected service account token volume. This projection can be used to insert a service account token into the pods runtime filesystem for use against APIs (Kubernetes API Server or otherwise).

entries.title: ServiceList
entries.summary: A list of Services in a cluster.

entries.title: ServicePort
entries.summary: ServicePort contains information on service's port.

entries.title: ServiceSpec
entries.summary: ServiceSpec describes the attributes that a user creates on a service.

entries.title: ServiceStatus
entries.summary: ServiceStatus represents the current status of a service.

entries.title: SessionAffinityConfig
entries.summary: SessionAffinityConfig represents the configurations of session affinity.

entries.title: SleepAction
entries.summary: The SleepAction API object represents a sleep action that can be executed by a Kubernetes Job.

entries.title: StorageOSPersistentVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a StorageOS persistent volume resource.

entries.title: StorageOSVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a StorageOS persistent volume resource.

entries.title: Sysctl
entries.summary: Sysctl defines a kernel parameter to be set

entries.title: TCPSocketAction
entries.summary: TCPSocketAction describes an action based on opening a socket

entries.title: Taint
entries.summary: The node this Taint is attached to has the "effect" on any pod that does not tolerate the Taint.

entries.title: Toleration
entries.summary: The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.

entries.title: TopologySelectorLabelRequirement
entries.summary: A topology selector requirement is a selector that matches given label. This is an alpha feature and may change in the future.

entries.title: TopologySelectorTerm
entries.summary: A topology selector term represents the result of label queries. A null or empty topology selector term matches no objects. The requirements of them are ANDed. It provides a subset of functionality as NodeSelectorTerm. This is an alpha feature and may change in the future.

entries.title: TopologySpreadConstraint
entries.summary: TopologySpreadConstraint specifies how to spread matching pods among the given topology.

entries.title: TypedLocalObjectReference
entries.summary: TypedLocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the typed referenced object inside the same namespace.

entries.title: TypedObjectReference
entries.summary: TypedObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the typed referenced object

entries.title: Volume
entries.summary: Volume represents a named volume in a pod that may be accessed by any container in the pod.

entries.title: VolumeDevice
entries.summary: volumeDevice describes a mapping of a raw block device within a container.

entries.title: VolumeMount
entries.summary: VolumeMount describes a mounting of a Volume within a container.

entries.title: VolumeMountStatus
entries.summary: VolumeMountStatus shows status of volume mounts.

entries.title: VolumeNodeAffinity
entries.summary: VolumeNodeAffinity defines constraints that limit what nodes this volume can be accessed from.

entries.title: VolumeProjection
entries.summary: Projection that may be projected along with other supported volume types. Exactly one of these fields must be set.

entries.title: VolumeResourceRequirements
entries.summary: VolumeResourceRequirements describes the storage resource requirements for a volume.

entries.title: VolumeStatus
entries.summary: VolumeStatus represents the status of a mounted volume. At most one of its members must be specified.

entries.title: VsphereVirtualDiskVolumeSource
entries.summary: Represents a virtual disk volume source for vSphere.

entries.title: WeightedPodAffinityTerm
entries.summary: The weights of all of the matched WeightedPodAffinityTerm fields are added per-node to find the most preferred node(s)

entries.title: WindowsSecurityContextOptions
entries.summary: WindowsSecurityContextOptions contain Windows-specific options and credentials.

entries.title: Endpoint
entries.summary: Endpoint represents a single logical "backend" implementing a service.

entries.title: EndpointConditions
entries.summary: EndpointConditions represents the current condition of an endpoint.

entries.title: EndpointHints
entries.summary: EndpointHints provides hints describing how an endpoint should be consumed.

entries.title: EndpointSlice
entries.summary: EndpointSlice represents a set of service endpoints. Most EndpointSlices are created by the EndpointSlice controller to represent the Pods selected by Service objects. For a given service there may be multiple EndpointSlice objects which must be joined to produce the full set of endpoints; you can find all of the slices for a given service by listing EndpointSlices in the service's namespace whose `kubernetes.io/service-name` label contains the service's name.

entries.title: EndpointSliceList
entries.summary: EndpointSliceList represents a list of endpoint slices

entries.title: ForNode
entries.summary: ForNode provides information about which nodes should consume this endpoint.

entries.title: ForZone
entries.summary: ForZone provides information about which zones should consume this endpoint.

entries.title: ExemptPriorityLevelConfiguration
entries.summary: ExemptPriorityLevelConfiguration describes the configurable aspects of the handling of exempt requests. In the mandatory exempt configuration object the values in the fields here can be modified by authorized users, unlike the rest of the `spec`.

entries.title: FlowDistinguisherMethod
entries.summary: FlowDistinguisherMethod specifies the method of a flow distinguisher.

entries.title: FlowSchema
entries.summary: FlowSchema defines the schema of a group of flows. Note that a flow is made up of a set of inbound API requests with similar attributes and is identified by a pair of strings: the name of the FlowSchema and a "flow distinguisher".

entries.title: FlowSchemaCondition
entries.summary: FlowSchemaCondition describes conditions for a FlowSchema.

entries.title: FlowSchemaList
entries.summary: FlowSchemaList is a list of FlowSchema objects.

entries.title: FlowSchemaSpec
entries.summary: FlowSchemaSpec describes how the FlowSchema's specification looks like.

entries.title: FlowSchemaStatus
entries.summary: FlowSchemaStatus represents the current state of a FlowSchema.

entries.title: GroupSubject
entries.summary: GroupSubject holds detailed information for group-kind subject.

entries.title: LimitResponse
entries.summary: LimitResponse defines how to handle requests that can not be executed right now.

entries.title: LimitedPriorityLevelConfiguration
entries.summary:
LimitedPriorityLevelConfiguration specifies how to handle requests that are subject to limits. It addresses two issues:
  - How are requests for this priority level limited?
  - What should be done with requests that exceed the limit?

entries.title: NonResourcePolicyRule
entries.summary: NonResourcePolicyRule is a predicate that matches non-resource requests according to their verb and the target non-resource URL. A NonResourcePolicyRule matches a request if and only if both (a) at least one member of verbs matches the request and (b) at least one member of nonResourceURLs matches the request.

entries.title: PolicyRulesWithSubjects
entries.summary: PolicyRulesWithSubjects prescribes a test that applies to a request to an apiserver. The test considers the subject making the request, the verb being requested, and the resource to be acted upon. This PolicyRulesWithSubjects matches a request if and only if both (a) at least one member of subjects matches the request and (b) at least one member of resourceRules or nonResourceRules matches the request.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfiguration
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfiguration represents the configuration of a priority level.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfigurationCondition
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfigurationCondition defines the condition of priority level.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfigurationList
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfigurationList is a list of PriorityLevelConfiguration objects.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfigurationReference
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfigurationReference contains information that points to the "request-priority" being used.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec specifies the configuration of a priority level.

entries.title: PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus
entries.summary: PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus represents the current state of a "request-priority".

entries.title: QueuingConfiguration
entries.summary: QueuingConfiguration holds the configuration parameters for queuing

entries.title: ResourcePolicyRule
entries.summary: ResourcePolicyRule is a predicate that matches some resource requests, testing the request's verb and the target resource. A ResourcePolicyRule matches a resource request if and only if: (a) at least one member of verbs matches the request, (b) at least one member of apiGroups matches the request, (c) at least one member of resources matches the request, and (d) either (d1) the request does not specify a namespace (i.e., `Namespace==""`) and clusterScope is true or (d2) the request specifies a namespace and least one member of namespaces matches the request's namespace.

entries.title: ServiceAccountSubject
entries.summary: ServiceAccountSubject holds detailed information for service-account-kind subject.

entries.title: Subject
entries.summary: Subject matches the originator of a request, as identified by the request authentication system. There are three ways of matching an originator; by user, group, or service account.

entries.title: UserSubject
entries.summary: UserSubject holds detailed information for user-kind subject.

entries.title: HTTPIngressPath
entries.summary: HTTPIngressPath associates a path with a backend. Incoming urls matching the path are forwarded to the backend.

entries.title: HTTPIngressRuleValue
entries.summary: HTTPIngressRuleValue is a list of http selectors pointing to backends. In the example: http://<host>/<path>?<searchpart> -> backend where where parts of the url correspond to RFC 3986, this resource will be used to match against everything after the last '/' and before the first '?' or '#'.

entries.title: IPAddress
entries.summary: IPAddress represents a single IP of a single IP Family. The object is designed to be used by APIs that operate on IP addresses. The object is used by the Service core API for allocation of IP addresses. An IP address can be represented in different formats, to guarantee the uniqueness of the IP, the name of the object is the IP address in canonical format, four decimal digits separated by dots suppressing leading zeros for IPv4 and the representation defined by RFC 5952 for IPv6. Valid: 192.168.1.5 or 2001:db8::1 or 2001:db8:aaaa:bbbb:cccc:dddd:eeee:1 Invalid: 10.01.2.3 or 2001:db8:0:0:0::1

entries.title: IPAddressList
entries.summary: IPAddressList contains a list of IPAddress.

entries.title: IPAddressSpec
entries.summary: IPAddressSpec describe the attributes in an IP Address.

entries.title: IPBlock
entries.summary: IPBlock describes a particular CIDR (Ex. "192.168.1.0/24","2001:db8::/64") that is allowed to the pods matched by a NetworkPolicySpec's podSelector. The except entry describes CIDRs that should not be included within this rule.

entries.title: Ingress
entries.summary: Ingress is a collection of rules that allow inbound connections to reach the endpoints defined by a backend. An Ingress can be configured to give services externally-reachable urls, load balance traffic, terminate SSL, offer name based virtual hosting etc.

entries.title: IngressBackend
entries.summary: IngressBackend describes all endpoints for a given service and port.

entries.title: IngressClass
entries.summary: IngressClass represents the class of the Ingress, referenced by the Ingress Spec. The `ingressclass.kubernetes.io/is-default-class` annotation can be used to indicate that an IngressClass should be considered default. When a single IngressClass resource has this annotation set to true, new Ingress resources without a class specified will be assigned this default class.

entries.title: IngressClassList
entries.summary: IngressClassList is a collection of IngressClasses.

entries.title: IngressClassParametersReference
entries.summary: IngressClassParametersReference identifies an API object. This can be used to specify a cluster or namespace-scoped resource.

entries.title: IngressClassSpec
entries.summary: IngressClassSpec provides information about the class of an Ingress.

entries.title: IngressList
entries.summary: A list of Ingress resources.

entries.title: IngressLoadBalancerIngress
entries.summary: IngressLoadBalancerIngress represents the status of a load-balancer ingress point.

entries.title: IngressLoadBalancerStatus
entries.summary: IngressLoadBalancerStatus represents the status of a load-balancer.

entries.title: IngressPortStatus
entries.summary: IngressPortStatus represents the error condition of a service port

entries.title: IngressRule
entries.summary: IngressRule represents the rules mapping the paths under a specified host to the related backend services. Incoming requests are first evaluated for a host match, then routed to the backend associated with the matching IngressRuleValue.

entries.title: IngressServiceBackend
entries.summary: IngressServiceBackend references a Kubernetes Service as a Backend.

entries.title: IngressSpec
entries.summary: IngressSpec describes the Ingress the user wishes to exist.

entries.title: IngressStatus
entries.summary: IngressStatus describe the current state of the Ingress.

entries.title: IngressTLS
entries.summary: IngressTLS describes the transport layer security associated with an ingress.

entries.title: NetworkPolicy
entries.summary: NetworkPolicy describes what network traffic is allowed for a set of Pods

entries.title: NetworkPolicyEgressRule
entries.summary: NetworkPolicyEgressRule describes a particular set of traffic that is allowed out of pods matched by a NetworkPolicySpec's podSelector. The traffic must match both ports and to. This type is beta-level in 1.8

entries.title: NetworkPolicyIngressRule
entries.summary: NetworkPolicyIngressRule describes a particular set of traffic that is allowed to the pods matched by a NetworkPolicySpec's podSelector. The traffic must match both ports and from.

entries.title: NetworkPolicyList
entries.summary: NetworkPolicyList is a list of NetworkPolicy objects.

entries.title: NetworkPolicyPeer
entries.summary: NetworkPolicyPeer describes a peer to allow traffic to/from. Only certain combinations of fields are allowed

entries.title: NetworkPolicyPort
entries.summary: NetworkPolicyPort describes a port to allow traffic on

entries.title: NetworkPolicySpec
entries.summary: NetworkPolicySpec provides the specification of a NetworkPolicy

entries.title: ParentReference
entries.summary: ParentReference describes a reference to a parent object.

entries.title: ServiceBackendPort
entries.summary: ServiceBackendPort is the service port being referenced.

entries.title: ServiceCIDR
entries.summary: ServiceCIDR defines a range of IP addresses using CIDR format (e.g. 192.168.0.0/24 or 2001:db2::/64). This range is used to allocate ClusterIPs to Service objects.

entries.title: ServiceCIDRList
entries.summary: ServiceCIDRList contains a list of ServiceCIDR objects.

entries.title: ServiceCIDRSpec
entries.summary: ServiceCIDRSpec define the CIDRs the user wants to use for allocating ClusterIPs for Services.

entries.title: ServiceCIDRStatus
entries.summary: ServiceCIDRStatus describes the current state of the ServiceCIDR.

entries.title: Overhead
entries.summary: Overhead structure represents the resource overhead associated with running a pod.

entries.title: RuntimeClass
entries.summary: RuntimeClass defines a class of container runtime supported in the cluster. The RuntimeClass is used to determine which container runtime is used to run all containers in a pod. RuntimeClasses are manually defined by a user or cluster provisioner, and referenced in the PodSpec. The Kubelet is responsible for resolving the RuntimeClassName reference before running the pod.  For more details, see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/runtime-class/

entries.title: RuntimeClassList
entries.summary: RuntimeClassList is a list of RuntimeClass objects.

entries.title: Scheduling
entries.summary: Scheduling specifies the scheduling constraints for nodes supporting a RuntimeClass.

entries.title: Eviction
entries.summary: Eviction evicts a pod from its node subject to certain policies and safety constraints. This is a subresource of Pod.  A request to cause such an eviction is created by POSTing to .../pods/<pod name>/evictions.

entries.title: PodDisruptionBudget
entries.summary: PodDisruptionBudget is an object to define the max disruption that can be caused to a collection of pods

entries.title: PodDisruptionBudgetList
entries.summary: PodDisruptionBudgetList is a collection of PodDisruptionBudgets.

entries.title: PodDisruptionBudgetSpec
entries.summary: PodDisruptionBudgetSpec is a description of a PodDisruptionBudget.

entries.title: PodDisruptionBudgetStatus
entries.summary: PodDisruptionBudgetStatus represents information about the status of a PodDisruptionBudget. Status may trail the actual state of a system.

entries.title: AggregationRule
entries.summary: AggregationRule describes how to locate ClusterRoles to aggregate into the ClusterRole

entries.title: ClusterRole
entries.summary: ClusterRole is a cluster level, logical grouping of PolicyRules that can be referenced as a unit by a RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding.

entries.title: ClusterRoleBinding
entries.summary: ClusterRoleBinding references a ClusterRole, but not contain it.  It can reference a ClusterRole in the global namespace, and adds who information via Subject.

entries.title: ClusterRoleBindingList
entries.summary: ClusterRoleBindingList is a collection of ClusterRoleBindings

entries.title: ClusterRoleList
entries.summary: ClusterRoleList is a collection of ClusterRoles

entries.title: PolicyRule
entries.summary: PolicyRule holds information that describes a policy rule, but does not contain information about who the rule applies to or which namespace the rule applies to.

entries.title: Role
entries.summary: Role is a namespaced, logical grouping of PolicyRules that can be referenced as a unit by a RoleBinding.

entries.title: RoleBinding
entries.summary: RoleBinding references a role, but does not contain it.  It can reference a Role in the same namespace or a ClusterRole in the global namespace. It adds who information via Subjects and namespace information by which namespace it exists in.  RoleBindings in a given namespace only have effect in that namespace.

entries.title: RoleBindingList
entries.summary: RoleBindingList is a collection of RoleBindings

entries.title: RoleList
entries.summary: A RoleList is an API object that represents a list of roles in a Kubernetes cluster.

entries.title: RoleRef
entries.summary: RoleRef contains information that points to the role being used

entries.title: AllocatedDeviceStatus
entries.summary:
AllocatedDeviceStatus contains the status of an allocated device, if the driver chooses to report it. This may include driver-specific information.

The combination of Driver, Pool, Device, and ShareID must match the corresponding key in Status.Allocation.Devices.

entries.title: AllocationResult
entries.summary: AllocationResult contains attributes of an allocated resource.

entries.title: CELDeviceSelector
entries.summary: CELDeviceSelector contains a CEL expression for selecting a device.

entries.title: CapacityRequestPolicy
entries.summary:
CapacityRequestPolicy defines how requests consume device capacity.

Must not set more than one ValidRequestValues.

entries.title: CapacityRequestPolicyRange
entries.summary:
CapacityRequestPolicyRange defines a valid range for consumable capacity values.

  - If the requested amount is less than Min, it is rounded up to the Min value.
  - If Step is set and the requested amount is between Min and Max but not aligned with Step,
    it will be rounded up to the next value equal to Min + (n * Step).
  - If Step is not set, the requested amount is used as-is if it falls within the range Min to Max (if set).
  - If the requested or rounded amount exceeds Max (if set), the request does not satisfy the policy,
    and the device cannot be allocated.

entries.title: CapacityRequirements
entries.summary: CapacityRequirements defines the capacity requirements for a specific device request.

entries.title: Counter
entries.summary: Counter describes a quantity associated with a device.

entries.title: CounterSet
entries.summary:
CounterSet defines a named set of counters that are available to be used by devices defined in the ResourcePool.

The counters are not allocatable by themselves, but can be referenced by devices. When a device is allocated, the portion of counters it uses will no longer be available for use by other devices.

entries.title: Device
entries.summary: Device represents one individual hardware instance that can be selected based on its attributes. Besides the name, exactly one field must be set.

entries.title: DeviceAllocationConfiguration
entries.summary: DeviceAllocationConfiguration gets embedded in an AllocationResult.

entries.title: DeviceAllocationResult
entries.summary: DeviceAllocationResult is the result of allocating devices.

entries.title: DeviceAttribute
entries.summary: DeviceAttribute must have exactly one field set.

entries.title: DeviceCapacity
entries.summary: DeviceCapacity describes a quantity associated with a device.

entries.title: DeviceClaim
entries.summary: DeviceClaim defines how to request devices with a ResourceClaim.

entries.title: DeviceClaimConfiguration
entries.summary: DeviceClaimConfiguration is used for configuration parameters in DeviceClaim.

entries.title: DeviceClass
entries.summary: DeviceClass is a vendor- or admin-provided resource that contains device configuration and selectors. It can be referenced in the device requests of a claim to apply these presets. Cluster scoped.

entries.title: DeviceClassConfiguration
entries.summary: DeviceClassConfiguration is used in DeviceClass.

entries.title: DeviceClassList
entries.summary: DeviceClassList is a collection of classes.

entries.title: DeviceClassSpec
entries.summary: DeviceClassSpec is used in a [DeviceClass] to define what can be allocated and how to configure it.

entries.title: DeviceConstraint
entries.summary: DeviceConstraint must have exactly one field set besides Requests.

entries.title: DeviceCounterConsumption
entries.summary: DeviceCounterConsumption defines a set of counters that a device will consume from a CounterSet.

entries.title: DeviceRequest
entries.summary: DeviceRequest is a request for devices required for a claim. This is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can also ask for several identical devices. With FirstAvailable it is also possible to provide a prioritized list of requests.

entries.title: DeviceRequestAllocationResult
entries.summary: DeviceRequestAllocationResult contains the allocation result for one request.

entries.title: DeviceSelector
entries.summary: DeviceSelector must have exactly one field set.

entries.title: DeviceSubRequest
entries.summary:
DeviceSubRequest describes a request for device provided in the claim.spec.devices.requests[].firstAvailable array. Each is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can also ask for several identical devices.

DeviceSubRequest is similar to ExactDeviceRequest, but doesn't expose the AdminAccess field as that one is only supported when requesting a specific device.

entries.title: DeviceTaint
entries.summary: The device this taint is attached to has the "effect" on any claim which does not tolerate the taint and, through the claim, to pods using the claim.

entries.title: DeviceTaintRule
entries.summary: DeviceTaintRule adds one taint to all devices which match the selector. This has the same effect as if the taint was specified directly in the ResourceSlice by the DRA driver.

entries.title: DeviceTaintRuleList
entries.summary: DeviceTaintRuleList is a collection of DeviceTaintRules.

entries.title: DeviceTaintRuleSpec
entries.summary: DeviceTaintRuleSpec specifies the selector and one taint.

entries.title: DeviceTaintRuleStatus
entries.summary: DeviceTaintRuleStatus provides information about an on-going pod eviction.

entries.title: DeviceTaintSelector
entries.summary: DeviceTaintSelector defines which device(s) a DeviceTaintRule applies to. The empty selector matches all devices. Without a selector, no devices are matched.

entries.title: DeviceToleration
entries.summary: The ResourceClaim this DeviceToleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.

entries.title: ExactDeviceRequest
entries.summary: ExactDeviceRequest is a request for one or more identical devices.

entries.title: NetworkDeviceData
entries.summary: NetworkDeviceData provides network-related details for the allocated device. This information may be filled by drivers or other components to configure or identify the device within a network context.

entries.title: NodeAllocatableResourceMapping
entries.summary: NodeAllocatableResourceMapping defines the translation between the DRA device/capacity units requested to the corresponding quantity of the node allocatable resource.

entries.title: OpaqueDeviceConfiguration
entries.summary: OpaqueDeviceConfiguration contains configuration parameters for a driver in a format defined by the driver vendor.

entries.title: ResourceClaimConsumerReference
entries.summary: ResourceClaimConsumerReference contains enough information to let you locate the consumer of a ResourceClaim. The user must be a resource in the same namespace as the ResourceClaim.

entries.title: ResourceClaimList
entries.summary: ResourceClaimList is a collection of claims.

entries.title: ResourceClaimSpec
entries.summary: ResourceClaimSpec defines what is being requested in a ResourceClaim and how to configure it.

entries.title: ResourceClaimStatus
entries.summary: ResourceClaimStatus tracks whether the resource has been allocated and what the result of that was.

entries.title: ResourceClaimTemplate
entries.summary: ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects.

entries.title: ResourceClaimTemplateList
entries.summary: ResourceClaimTemplateList is a collection of claim templates.

entries.title: ResourceClaimTemplateSpec
entries.summary: ResourceClaimTemplateSpec contains the metadata and fields for a ResourceClaim.

entries.title: ResourcePool
entries.summary: ResourcePool describes the pool that ResourceSlices belong to.

entries.title: ResourceSlice
entries.summary:
ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources, managed by a common driver. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.

At the moment, the only supported resources are devices with attributes and capacities. Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name. The ResourceSlice in which a device gets published may change over time. The unique identifier for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.

Whenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number and updates all ResourceSlices with that new number and new resource definitions. A consumer must only use ResourceSlices with the highest generation number and ignore all others.

When allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when looking for the best solution among several different alternatives, a consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.

For resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead, the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.

entries.title: ResourceSliceList
entries.summary: ResourceSliceList is a collection of ResourceSlices.

entries.title: ResourceSliceSpec
entries.summary: ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice.

entries.title: PoolStatus
entries.summary: PoolStatus contains status information for a single resource pool.

entries.title: ResourcePoolStatusRequest
entries.summary: ResourcePoolStatusRequest triggers a one-time calculation of resource pool status based on the provided filters. Once status is set, the request is considered complete and will not be reprocessed. Users should delete and recreate requests to get updated information.

entries.title: ResourcePoolStatusRequestList
entries.summary: ResourcePoolStatusRequestList is a collection of ResourcePoolStatusRequests.

entries.title: ResourcePoolStatusRequestSpec
entries.summary: ResourcePoolStatusRequestSpec defines the filters for the pool status request.

entries.title: ResourcePoolStatusRequestStatus
entries.summary: ResourcePoolStatusRequestStatus contains the calculated pool status information.

entries.title: BasicDevice
entries.summary: BasicDevice defines one device instance.

entries.title: PriorityClass
entries.summary: PriorityClass defines mapping from a priority class name to the priority integer value. The value can be any valid integer.

entries.title: PriorityClassList
entries.summary: PriorityClassList is a collection of priority classes.

entries.title: AllDisruptionMode
entries.summary: AllDisruptionMode specifies that children can only be disrupted together.

entries.title: BasicSchedulingPolicy
entries.summary: BasicSchedulingPolicy indicates that standard Kubernetes scheduling behavior should be used.

entries.title: CompositeBasicSchedulingPolicy
entries.summary: CompositeBasicSchedulingPolicy indicates that the groups belonging to the composite group should be scheduled independently.

entries.title: CompositeGangSchedulingPolicy
entries.summary: CompositeGangSchedulingPolicy indicates that the groups belonging to the composite group should be scheduled using all-or-nothing semantics.

entries.title: CompositePodGroup
entries.summary: CompositePodGroup represents a runtime instance of pod groups grouped together. CompositePodGroups are created by workload controllers (LWS, JobSet, etc...) from Workload.compositePodGroupTemplates. CompositePodGroup API enablement is toggled by the CompositePodGroup feature gate.

entries.title: CompositePodGroupList
entries.summary: CompositePodGroupList contains a list of CompositePodGroup resources.

entries.title: CompositePodGroupSchedulingPolicy
entries.summary: CompositePodGroupSchedulingPolicy defines the scheduling configuration for a CompositePodGroup. Exactly one policy must be set.

entries.title: CompositePodGroupSpec
entries.summary: CompositePodGroupSpec defines the desired state of CompositePodGroup.

entries.title: CompositePodGroupStatus
entries.summary: CompositePodGroupStatus represents information about the status of a composite pod group.

entries.title: CompositePodGroupTemplate
entries.summary: CompositePodGroupTemplate represents a template for a CompositePodGroup with a scheduling policy.

entries.title: DisruptionMode
entries.summary: DisruptionMode defines how individual entities within a group can be disrupted. Exactly one mode can be set.

entries.title: GangSchedulingPolicy
entries.summary: GangSchedulingPolicy defines the parameters for gang scheduling.

entries.title: PodGroup
entries.summary: PodGroup represents a runtime instance of pods grouped together. PodGroups are created by workload controllers (Job, LWS, JobSet, etc...) from Workload.podGroupTemplates. PodGroup API enablement is toggled by the GenericWorkload feature gate.

entries.title: PodGroupList
entries.summary: PodGroupList contains a list of PodGroup resources.

entries.title: PodGroupResourceClaim
entries.summary:
PodGroupResourceClaim references exactly one ResourceClaim, either directly or by naming a ResourceClaimTemplate which is then turned into a ResourceClaim for the PodGroup.

It adds a name to it that uniquely identifies the ResourceClaim inside the PodGroup. Pods that need access to the ResourceClaim define a matching reference in its own Spec.ResourceClaims. The Pod's claim must match all fields of the PodGroup's claim exactly.

entries.title: PodGroupResourceClaimStatus
